en.extract:
The Chinese francolin (Francolinus pintadeanus) or Burmese francolin is a species of game bird in the family Phasianidae.


Description
On average, the length of the bird is 30–34 cm (12–13 in) and it weighs 280–400 g (9.9–14.1 oz). Females are slightly smaller than males.


Distribution and habitat
This species is found in Cambodia, China, India, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, and Vietnam. Introduced to Mauritius, the Philippines, Madagascar, United States, Chile and Argentina. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forest and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest.


References

The '''Chinese francolin''' ('''''Francolinus pintadeanus''''') or '''Burmese francolin''' is a [[species]] of game [[bird]] in the family [[Phasianidae]].

==Description==
On average, the length of the bird is {{convert|30|-|34|cm|abbr=on}} and it weighs {{convert|280|-|400|g|abbr=on}}. Females are slightly smaller than males.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Hume|first1=A.O.|last2=Marshall|first2=C.H.T.|title=Game Birds of India, Burmah and Ceylon|year=1880|publisher=A.O. Hume and C.H.T. Marshall|location=Calcutta|page=30|volume=II}}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
This species is found in [[Cambodia]], [[China]], [[India]], [[Laos]], [[Myanmar]], [[Thailand]], and [[Vietnam]]. Introduced to [[Mauritius]], the [[Philippines]], [[Madagascar]], [[United States]], [[Chile]] and [[Argentina]].<ref>Long, John L. (1981). Introduced Birds of the World. Agricultural Protection Board of Western Australia, 21-493</ref> Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical dry forest]] and [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]].
